DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
accessibility artifact-reference example extensions guideline link metadata rich-text status usecase
anatomy api component design-specifications state variant
token-api token-value token
style
pattern

DSDS Component Definitions

Definitions for the composite component document.

Source: components/component.schema.json

componentDoc

Documentation for a single reusable UI component.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
namestringrequiredMachine-readable identifier (e.g., 'button', 'dialog', 'form-field', 'data-table').
Pattern: ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$
displayNamestringrequiredHuman-readable name.
descriptionrichTextrequired
statusstatusrequired
aliasesstring[]optionalAlternative names this component is known by across teams, tools, or legacy systems (e.g., ['btn', 'action-button', 'CTA']). Useful for search, migration mapping, and cross-referencing when different disciplines use different terminology for the same component.
summarystringoptionalA one-line, plain-text summary. MUST NOT contain markup.
deprecationNoticestringoptionalRequired when status is 'deprecated'.
sincestringoptionalThe version in which this component was introduced.
tagsstring[]optionalFreeform keywords to relate guidelines across categories/disciplines (e.g., 'rtl', 'localization', 'validation').
categorystringoptionalComponent classification.
Pattern: ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$
previewexample[]optionalVisual previews of the component.
anatomyanatomyoptional
apicomponentApi | map<string, componentApi>optionalThe code-level API. Single API object or platform-keyed map.
variantsvariant[]optionalNamed visual/behavioral configurations.
statesstate[]optionalInteractive states.
designSpecificationsdesignSpecificationsoptional
useCasesuseCasesoptional
guidelinesguideline[]optionalUsage guidelines for the component. Each entry is a self-contained guideline. Use the `category` property on individual guidelines to group by concern (e.g., 'design', 'accessibility', 'content').
accessibilityaccessibilityObjectoptional
linkslink[]optionalExternal resource links and related artifact references.
$extensionsextensionsoptional

Conditional: When status is "deprecated", then deprecationNotice is required.

References: richText, status, example, anatomy, componentApi, variant, state, designSpecifications, useCases, guideline, accessibilityObject, link, extensions

Example

{
  "name": "button",
  "displayName": "Button",
  "description": "An interactive element that triggers an action when activated. Buttons communicate what will happen when the user interacts with them.",
  "status": "stable",
  "since": "1.0.0",
  "category": "action",
  "aliases": [
    "btn",
    "action-button"
  ],
  "summary": "An interactive element that triggers an action when activated.",
  "tags": [
    "action",
    "form",
    "interactive"
  ],
  "useCases": {
    "whenToUse": [
      {
        "description": "When the user needs to trigger an action such as submitting a form, saving data, or opening a dialog."
      },
      {
        "description": "When a destructive or irreversible action needs to be initiated, such as deleting a record. Pair with a confirmation dialog."
      }
    ],
    "whenNotToUse": [
      {
        "description": "When the action navigates the user to a different page or URL.",
        "alternative": {
          "name": "link",
          "rationale": "Links carry native navigation semantics. Screen readers announce them as links, and browsers support standard navigation behaviors such as open-in-new-tab."
        }
      },
      {
        "description": "When the only content is an icon with no visible text label.",
        "alternative": {
          "name": "icon-button",
          "rationale": "Icon buttons enforce an aria-label requirement and apply size adjustments for icon-only touch targets."
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  "guidelines": [
    {
      "guidance": "Limit each surface to one primary button.",
      "rationale": "Multiple primary buttons dilute visual hierarchy. When everything is emphasized, nothing is.",
      "type": "required",
      "category": "visual-design"
    },
    {
      "guidance": "Use the loading state instead of disabling the button during asynchronous operations.",
      "rationale": "A disabled button gives no feedback that an action is in progress. The loading state communicates that the action was registered and the system is working.",
      "type": "encouraged",
      "category": "interaction"
    },
    {
      "guidance": "When using an icon-only button, provide an aria-label that describes the action.",
      "rationale": "Screen readers announce button content as the accessible name. Without visible text, there is no accessible name.",
      "type": "required",
      "category": "accessibility",
      "criteria": [
        "https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#name-role-value"
      ]
    },
    {
      "guidance": "Use a verb or verb phrase that describes the action the button performs. Two words maximum.",
      "rationale": "Action-oriented labels set clear expectations about what will happen on activation. Short labels prevent truncation.",
      "type": "required",
      "category": "content",
      "target": "label"
    }
  ],
  "accessibility": {
    "wcagLevel": "AA",
    "keyboardInteraction": [
      {
        "key": "Enter",
        "action": "Activates the button."
      },
      {
        "key": "Space",
        "action": "Activates the button."
      }
    ],
    "ariaAttributes": [
      {
        "attribute": "aria-disabled",
        "value": "true | false",
        "description": "Set to true when the button is non-interactive. Preferred over the HTML disabled attribute when the button should remain focusable.",
        "required": false
      },
      {
        "attribute": "aria-label",
        "value": "string",
        "description": "Provides an accessible name for icon-only buttons.",
        "required": false
      }
    ],
    "screenReaderBehavior": "Announced as '[label], button'. When disabled via aria-disabled, announced as '[label], button, dimmed'.",
    "focusManagement": "The button participates in the normal tab order. It does not trap or redirect focus."
  },
  "links": [
    {
      "type": "source",
      "url": "https://code.acme.com/design-system/src/components/button/button.tsx",
      "label": "Source code"
    },
    {
      "type": "documentation",
      "url": "https://design.acme.com/components/button",
      "label": "Documentation"
    },
    {
      "type": "alternative",
      "url": "https://design.acme.com/components/link",
      "label": "Link component"
    },
    {
      "type": "alternative",
      "url": "https://design.acme.com/components/icon-button",
      "label": "Icon Button component"
    }
  ]
}
↑ Back to top

Design System Documentation Standard (DSDS) 0.1 — Draft Specification

GitHub

On this page

  • componentDoc
